program.name: Westpac Group Graduate & Summer Intern Programs
program.salary_text: Each year, Westpac reviews graduate and intern salaries against the marketplace to make sure they are paid a competitive salary. Westpac also offers financial and lifestyle benefits and discounts, plus an employee share plan.
program.duration_text: Depending on the program, you may undertake a 12, 18 or 24-month rotational program.
program.rotation_text: You complete a rotational program across different roles, teams and project experiences within your business area.

eligibility.work_rights: You must be an Australian or New Zealand Citizen, or an Australian Permanent Resident, at the time of application.
eligibility.graduation_window: You can apply in your final year of your degree, or if you completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree within the past three years (at the time of application).
minimum_requirements: You must be in your final year of study of a university degree, or have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree within the past three years (at the time of application).
You must be an Australian or New Zealand Citizen, or an Australian Permanent Resident, at the time of application.

stages.name: Online application
stages.details: Complete the online application form (about 10 minutes).

stages.name: Online assessments
stages.details: If shortlisted, you are invited to complete an online psychometric assessment within 48 hours.

stages.name: Video interview
stages.details: A video interview to learn more about your motivations and your “Uncommon Mind”.

stages.name: Assessment centre
stages.details: You may be invited to attend an assessment centre to get to know Westpac and for Westpac to get to know you.

tips: Apply early because applications cannot be accepted after the closing date listed for your preferred program.
Use Westpac’s application toolkit and “Tips and Tricks” guide to help you prepare for the selection process and assessment centres.
Most assessment centres are held virtually, but some may be in person (Westpac will tell you in advance).
Prepare your resume and academic transcripts to submit with your application (PDF or Word format).

exec_themes: Major multi-year transformation program (UNITE) to simplify how the bank operates and improve efficiency and customer outcomes.
Service and customer experience improvement as a whole-of-bank priority (e.g., “one best way” approach, digital and process improvements).
Targeted growth focus, including momentum in business and institutional banking, while balancing growth and returns in mortgages.
Strengthening risk culture and meeting regulatory expectations as an ongoing strategic priority.
Investing in people (attraction, retention, leadership development) to support execution of strategy and transformation.
Embedding sustainability into the core strategy, with focus areas including climate transition, housing affordability, and regional prosperity.
commercial_context.profit_engine: Westpac mainly makes money by taking deposits and lending (especially mortgages, plus business and institutional lending) and earning net interest margin (the spread between interest earned and interest paid). It also earns non-interest income from banking services (for example, transaction-related and markets-related activities), with an emphasis on improving returns through efficiency and better service.
commercial_context.headwinds: Margin pressure is a key challenge (net interest margin is tracked closely and declined in some segments), alongside the cost and complexity of running a large transformation program (UNITE investment and higher amortisation). Competitive pressure for deposits and lending is implied by the strong focus on deposit growth and funding mix, but the presentation does not clearly label these as “headwinds.”
commercial_context.esg: Westpac’s sustainability strategy highlights three main focus areas: climate transition, housing affordability, and regional prosperity. Westpac states an ambition to become a net-zero, climate resilient bank, and describes its Climate Transition Plan as the way it will manage climate-related risks and opportunities and support customers’ transition. Westpac also positions “sustainable finance” as a way to deliver positive climate, environmental, and social outcomes through lending and bond facilitation activities under a Sustainable Finance Framework.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Westpac describes FY2025 as a “defining year” with renewed leadership and a clear growth strategy, and points to the first initiatives delivered under the UNITE transformation agenda. This suggests a pivot toward sharper execution, simplification, and targeted growth areas under new leadership, but the company does not present it as a single discrete pivot with a precise start date beyond the FY2025 framing.

notes: The graduate program open and close timings are only shown as months (not exact dates). Per extraction rules, months without a year were assumed to be in the current year (2026) and normalised to the first/last day of the month.

Westpac also states that 2027 Westpac Institutional Bank Graduate Program applications open on 16 March 2026, but this appears to be a specific subset rather than the general graduate program key dates table.
Citizenship/permanent residency and graduation timing are stated consistently across Westpac’s graduate pages.

sources.title: How to apply to our graduate program | Westpac

notes: The recruitment process is described as four steps on the graduate program page, with additional preparation guidance about toolkits and assessment centres.

sources.title: How to apply to our graduate program | Westpac

notes: Source PDFs for the 2025 Annual Report and 2025 Sustainability Report were not fully retrievable in this session due to a fetch timeout/error, so several items are drawn from the FY2025 Results Presentation & Investor Discussion Pack and Westpac sustainability webpages instead.

‘Headwinds’ are not explicitly listed as a section heading in the FY2025 results deck; the headwinds field is therefore summarised from clearly signposted pressures (margin movement, transformation investment and amortisation) rather than quoted ‘headwind’ language.

The ‘profit_engine’ field is expressed in plain English from standard bank mechanics and the deck’s focus on NIM, deposits, loans, and revenue lines; it is not presented as a single explicit sentence by Westpac in the sources accessed.
